name: Marko
description: An HTML-based streaming framework optimized for server-side rendering and progressive hydration at eBay scale

description.value: An HTML-based streaming framework optimized for server-side rendering and progressive hydration at eBay scale

initial-release.comment: Version 1.0 released in May 2014; created by Patrick Steele-Idem at eBay

current-version.comment: Marko 5 is current stable; Marko 6 is in development

active-maintenance.comment: Very active with 6,485 total commits, 125 contributors, and 948 releases

reactivity-model.comment: Compiler-based approach with fine-grained reactivity; Marko 6 adds signals-like primitives

component-model.comment: HTML-first single-file components with .marko extension; valid HTML is valid Marko

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative HTML-based templates with reactive extensions

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in reactivity with <let> tags and class-based component state

opinionatedness.comment: HTML-first philosophy with conventions but flexible integration

backward-compatibility.comment: Major versions introduce breaking changes; Marko 5 was a significant rewrite

primary-language.comment: Primary language is JavaScript (65.4% of codebase)

compiles-to.comment: Compiles .marko files to optimized JavaScript for server and browser

typescript-support.comment: Built-in TypeScript support with strong type inference across templates and components

template-syntax.comment: HTML-based syntax extended with conditionals, loops, and reactivity; almost any valid HTML is valid Marko

build-required.comment: Compiler-based approach requires a build step; uses Babel-based compiler

two-way-binding.comment: Event handlers with state updates for two-way binding patterns

event-handling.comment: Declarative event binding with onClick and on* handlers

list-rendering.comment: <for> tag for iteration over arrays and objects

component-slots.comment: Content projection via renderBody and named slots

suspense.comment: <await> tag for async loading with streaming support

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Direct DOM access via component lifecycle and refs

official-router.comment: Marko Run provides file-based routing as the official meta-framework

router-name.comment: Official meta-framework with file-based routing, layouts, and middleware

nested-routes.comment: Via Marko Run's directory-based routing with nested layouts

dynamic-routes.comment: Via Marko Run's $ prefix for dynamic segments

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Automatic code splitting in Marko Run

scroll-restoration.comment: Not documented in Marko Run; likely handled by browser defaults

built-in-state.comment: Built-in reactive state with <let> tags and class component state

built-in-global-state.comment: No official global state management; uses component state and context

global-state-library.comment: No official global state library; state is typically server-rendered or passed via context

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No dedicated Marko browser devtools extension found

official-cli.comment: Marko Run CLI for development and deployment

hot-module-replacement.comment: Live-reload during development via Vite integration

vs-code-extension.comment: Official Marko VSCode extension with syntax highlighting, TypeScript, and IntelliSense

error-messages.comment: Helpful compiler errors with component context; typical for HTML-based frameworks

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with interactive playground, guides, and TypeScript integration

interactive-tutorial.comment: No dedicated step-by-step interactive tutorial; playground serves as learning tool

bundle-size-core.comment: ~401 B minified + gzipped core runtime; actual apps ship minimal code via partial hydration

bundle-size-full.comment: ~10 kB typical for full apps; Marko emphasizes minimal client-side JavaScript

virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; uses direct DOM updates via fine-grained reactivity

compile-time-optimization.comment: Core to Marko's design; compiler generates optimized code for server and browser separately

tree-shaking.comment: Automatic code elimination at sub-template level

code-splitting.comment: Automatic code splitting with Marko Run

lazy-components.comment: Components can be loaded on demand via code splitting

ssr-capable.comment: SSR is Marko's primary strength; powers over a billion daily requests at eBay

streaming-ssr.comment: Pioneered streaming SSR in 2014; content streams to browser as soon as ready

partial-hydration.comment: Pioneered partial hydration in 2014; only ships code for interactive islands

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Marko is included in benchmarks but geometric mean varies; team notes synthetic benchmarks differ from real-world performance

component-libraries.comment: eBay UI Core (ebayui-core) is the primary component library

meta-framework.comment: Official meta-framework from the Marko team

major-companies-using.comment: eBay is the primary large-scale user; nearly 20,000 UI components at eBay

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Created at eBay; hosted by OpenJS Foundation since 2017

discord-community-size.comment: Discord server exists but member count not publicly documented

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Stack Overflow questions exist with 'marko' tag but count not verified

job-market-demand.comment: Niche market; primarily used at eBay with limited external adoption

testing-library-name.comment: Built on @testing-library/dom with Marko-specific render and cleanup APIs

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress, and other E2E tools

snapshot-testing.comment: Snapshot testing supported via Jest integration

mobile-framework.comment: No official native mobile framework; can use Capacitor or PhoneGap for hybrid apps

pwa-support.comment: PWA support via Marko Run and service workers

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; can use Tauri or Electron

learning-curve.comment: Gentle learning curve for those familiar with HTML; valid HTML is valid Marko

migration-path.comment: Migration guides available between major versions

incremental-adoption.comment: Can be integrated into existing Node.js applications; often used incrementally at eBay
